It gives your tears a chance to slow down enemies.
That same slow also affects any projectiles enemies fire at you.
90: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It reduces your movement speed as a tradeoff.
It's a nod to a Bible story where a modest object brings down a giant.
It grants +1 damage and a faster tear rate.
In the original game, it used to turn your tears grey.
91: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It also blocks certain projectiles that fall from above.
Its name and theme reference another indie exploration game.
It's grouped with Night Light as a light source, despite not actually glowing.
It reveals the room type of nearby rooms, up to 2 rooms away.
It can specifically reveal the location of Secret and Super Secret Rooms.
92: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It plays a unique pickup sound effect, one of only a few items that do.
It's named after a healing mechanic from the creator's other platformer.
Unlocked by collecting 4 copies of a related wound-care item in one run to form a special companion.
It grants one full Red Heart container plus 2 Soul Hearts.
Its pickup sound is lifted straight from that other game's healing pickup.
93: It is a Quality 1 Active Item.
Its name and design reference a classic handheld gaming console.
It transforms you into an invulnerable creature that instills fear in enemies.
Its look and pickup quote nod to a classic dot-munching arcade character.
94: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It works through a familiar that follows you, rather than an instant effect.
It's part of a themed set of carrying-container items, alongside Bomb Bag and Rune Bag.
Unlocked by defeating Isaac as Cain.
It spawns a familiar that drops a coin every 2 rooms.
It's the item used in the challenge PAY TO PLAY.
95: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It works through a passive familiar, not your own tears.
Its pickup quote trails off into a glitchy electronic noise.
It fires the same type of laser as the item Technology.
It spawns a floating familiar that shoots laser beams at enemies.
The familiar's design blends mechanical parts with the look of a small child.
106: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It grants 5 extra bombs immediately upon pickup.
Its name evokes something oversized, like an evolved video game form.
It boosts the size, blast radius, and damage of your bombs.
Its cosmetic effect on Isaac resembles that of a certain deadly sin.
107: It is a Quality 2 Active Item.
Its effect only lasts for the current room.
In real life, this tool has serrated blades used for cutting canvas.
In the original game, its unlock method instead unlocks Scissors in Rebirth.
It splits Isaac's head from his body, granting flight while the body fights alone.
Isaac's headless body resembles a Pacer enemy.
108: It is a Quality 4 Passive Item.
Its protection only kicks in above a certain damage threshold.
In real life, this thin object is consumed as a religious sacrament.
It can be found in both Item Rooms and Angel Rooms.
It caps big single hits at half a heart of damage.
Its pickup quote once had a word misspelled in the original game.
109: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
Its damage bonus maxes out at 99 collected coins.
Its name plays on a well-known saying equating wealth with influence.
It's the key item used in the PAY TO PLAY challenge.
It grants extra damage for every penny you're carrying.
At a full wallet of 99 coins, it deals almost 4 bonus damage.
110: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It's only a chance to trigger — not every tear freezes enemies.
In real life, this name refers to corrective lenses worn in the eye.
Unlocked by obtaining three Yes Mother? items in one run.
It adds a chance for your tears to freeze enemies solid.
Its appearance was redesigned in Booster Pack #5.
111: It is a Quality 0 Active Item.
It only affects enemies within close range.
Its pickup quote riffs on being able to pass gas whenever you like.
It can appear as a possible Beggar payout, not just in Item Rooms.
When used, Isaac releases a damaging, poisonous fart.
It recharges quickly, fully charging again in just 4 seconds.
112: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It deals 7 damage per tick to anything it touches.
The name comes from a religious belief in a personal protective spirit.
Unlocked by defeating Satan as Magdalene.
It spawns an orbiting familiar that blocks incoming enemy shots.
It also speeds up every orbital familiar you own, not just itself.
113: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It only attacks enemies that come within its reach.
In the original game, a different character was required to unlock it.
Unlocked by defeating The Lamb as Azazel.
It spawns a familiar that automatically fires at nearby enemies.
It follows Isaac closely, acting as a loyal little auto-turret.
114: It is a Quality 4 Passive Item.
Holding the fire button before releasing boosts its power.
In the original game, wielding it made Isaac resemble his mother's dress.
Unlocked by defeating Satan as Isaac.
It replaces your tears with a piercing blade that can hurt through obstacles.
Isaac wields it directly, stabbing enemies at melee range instead of shooting tears.
115: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It also gives a small boost to your tear rate.
In real life, this object is used to supposedly contact spirits, marked with letters and 'yes/no'.
It can appear in both Item Rooms and Devil Rooms.
Your tears become spectral, passing through obstacles but not enemies.
Isaac's freaked-out expression evokes a vengeful ghost from horror films.
